prompt: This previously normotensive patient has elevated blood pressure at today's visit. Please assess.
assertions: Classified as GESTATIONAL HYPERTENSION specifically (not pre-eclampsia)
Reasoning states: new-onset hypertension after 20 weeks + no proteinuria + no severe features
Does NOT over-diagnose as pre-eclampsia (critical discrimination test)
Danger signs counseled — specifically headache, visual changes, epigastric pain
Delivery planning at 37 weeks if condition remains stable
